title: Parent item mirror to subitem
excerpt: Seeing some idea submissions for this, but also looks like it used to be a capability. Is it possible to mirror something from the parent item into the subitem of the same board? (Bonus - without needing to create an Con…

posts.author: MondayMyco (K)
posts.cookedHtml:
Seeing some idea submissions for this, but also looks like it used to be a capability. Is it possible to mirror something from the parent item into the subitem of the same board? (Bonus - without needing to create an Connection column.)

Closed thread indicating past capability, but no how-to instructions: Connecting Item Board With Subitems Board - Get information from The parent item to the Subitem

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @MondayMyco - This is possible but only with using a connect board column and selecting the parent item to mirror the columns. There is also functionality in the Autoboost app to copy a value from the parent to the subitems.

image1232×817 80.3 KB

Hope this helps!
Mark

Green Llamas

monday.com + Green Llamas

Green Llamas is here to help you make all your People and Projects Green. Let us get your herds under control using the monday.com platform.

posts.author: Nir-Jetpack (Nir @ Jetpack Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
@MondayMyco Hi What you are trying to do is possible with VLOOKUP marketplace app by setting an auto-link between parent board and its sub-item board (this works since sub-items are a hidden sub board).

You do need a key value which both on board item and sub-item for matching between them.

Let me know if you need any help.
Nir

posts.author: workflowboost (Bartosz @ Workflow Boost)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,

Subitems Pro Automations app can help with that. You can use the following automation to copy parent column to all subitems:

image2358×640 33.7 KB

If you’re looking to copy a value to specific subitems, you can set up a connection using connected boards and mirror columns. If find a mirror column on subitem hard to work with, you can use a following automation to copy it to standard subitem column:

image2184×632 31.8 KB

If you have any questions or requests for custom automations, feel free to reach out to us!
